bugs.title: Year-end close zeroes out P&L (and Budget vs Actual / P&L by class / monthly P&L) for the closed year
bugs.evidence: fiscalClose.ts:201-209 posts a 'posted'-status closing entry dated `${fiscalYear}-12-31T23:59:59.000Z` that debits every revenue account and credits every expense account. profitAndLoss (lib/services/reports.ts:104-124) filters only on companyId/status='posted'/date — there is no exclusion for closing entries (no sourceRef/reference filter), and the closing timestamp falls inside any year-range `to` of 12-31T23:59:59.999Z. So after running year-end close, the P&L for that fiscal year reports $0 income/$0 expenses, December in profitAndLossByMonth shows huge offsetting negatives, and budgetVsActual actuals (budgets.ts:245-247 uses the same P&L) collapse to zero. QuickBooks Desktop never wipes closed-year P&L history.
bugs.fixSketch: Add a machine-readable closing-entry marker and exclude it from income-statement reports while keeping it in balance-sheet reports. Concretely: add an `is_closing_entry` boolean (default false) column to journal_entries (or finally persist the already-typed `sourceRef` field from posting.ts:38 as a column) via a Drizzle migration; have postJournalEntry write it and yearEndClose set it true. Then in profitAndLoss (reports.ts:104-124) and the other P&L-flavored queries that don't delegate to it (cashBasisReports profitAndLossCashBasis, classReports profitAndLossByClass) add `ne(journalEntries.isClosingEntry, true)` to the where-conds; profitAndLossByMonth/Comparative and budgetVsActual inherit the fix automatically since they call profitAndLoss. Leave trialBalance/balanceSheet (accountActivity) INCLUDING closing entries so Retained Earnings stays correct post-close — though note balanceSheet already folds net income into RE virtually, so verify it doesn't double-count RE after a real closing entry (it nets out because closed revenue/expense activity drops to zero, but add a test). Add a regression test: post 2024 activity, run yearEndClose(2024), then assert profitAndLoss({from: 2024-01-01, to: 2024-12-31T23:59:59.999Z}) still returns the original totals and budgetVsActual actuals are unchanged. A cleaner long-term alternative is the QuickBooks-style virtual close (no posted closing entry; compute RE rollover in balanceSheet), but the flag-and-filter approach is the minimal safe fix.

bugs.title: Cash Flow statement does not tie to cash: hardcoded account codes, missing liability/asset sections, and double-counts net income after year-end close
bugs.evidence: reportsExtra.ts:324-339 — operating adjustments only consider accounts with literal codes '1200', '2000', '1300'; investing only code '1500'; financing (lines 341-374) only equity-type accounts. Changes in loans, credit cards, payroll liabilities, sales-tax payable, other current/long-term liabilities, depreciation add-back, and any asset not coded 1500 are ignored, so `netCashChange` does not equal the actual change in cash whenever those move. Compounding: yearEndClose credits Retained Earnings (equity, code 3900), which this report then counts as a financing inflow equal to net income — double-counting it on top of operating. And qbImport.ts:253 derives account codes from ACCNUM/name, so for QB-imported companies the hardcoded-code lookups (line 295: 'account not in COA — report zero') silently return zero adjustments.
bugs.fixSketch: Rewrite cashFlow in lib/services/reportsExtra.ts to be classification-driven instead of code-driven: in one grouped query, compute each account's net debit/credit movement over the period from posted journal lines (excluding closing entries by sourceRef LIKE 'fiscal-close:%'), then classify by accounts.type/subtype rather than literal codes — cash = asset subtypes checking/savings/undeposited; operating adjustments = AR subtype accounts_receivable, inventory subtype, AP subtype accounts_payable, credit_card and other current liabilities (sales tax, payroll liabilities), plus a depreciation add-back from accumulated-depreciation/contra-asset movement; investing = fixed_assets (and other long-term asset) subtypes; financing = long-term debt subtypes plus equity (with the fiscal-close RE entry excluded so net income isn't echoed into financing). Compute netIncome from the same movement data (revenue/expense, close entries excluded) instead of calling profitAndLoss. Because every non-cash account is then bucketed into exactly one section, netCashChange ties to the cash-account movement by construction; add a test that posts a payroll check (2300), a taxable invoice + receipt (2200), a credit-card-funded bill payment (2100), a loan draw, and a year-end close, and asserts netCashChange equals the measured change in cash-subtype accounts.

bugs.title: A/R and A/P aging 'as of' a past date is wrong — uses live balanceDue and includes invoices/bills created after asOf
bugs.evidence: arAging (reportsExtra.ts:91-127) and apAging (141-175) take an `asOf` date but query the CURRENT `invoices.balanceDue`/`bills.balanceDue` with no `date <= asOf` filter and no reconstruction of the balance as of that date. A backdated aging therefore (a) reflects payments received after asOf, and (b) includes documents dated after asOf (bucketed as 'current' via negative daysPastDue at line 117). It also omits unapplied credit memos/credits as negative rows, so the AR total overstates net receivables vs QB's aging.
bugs.fixSketch: Reconstruct each document's balance as of the cutoff instead of trusting the live column. In arAging/apAging: (1) filter documents to `invoices.date <= asOf` (and status != 'void' only — do NOT exclude 'paid', since a now-paid invoice may have been open at asOf); (2) compute balanceAsOf = invoice total minus the sum of payment applications (and credit-memo applications) whose payment/credit date <= asOf — join the payment_applications/payments tables (and credit memo application tables) with a `payments.date <= asOf` filter, aggregating per invoice/bill; (3) keep rows where balanceAsOf > 0 and bucket by daysPastDue exactly as today; (4) optionally add unapplied credit memos dated <= asOf as negative 'current' rows per customer for QB parity, adjusting buildAgingReport totals accordingly. Mirror the same change for bills/bill payments in apAging, and add tests: invoice paid after asOf still appears in a backdated aging; invoice dated after asOf is excluded; unapplied credit memo reduces the customer total.

bugs.title: General Ledger report with a 'from' date has no opening balance — running balance and 'Closing Balance' are wrong
bugs.evidence: generalLedger (journal.ts:257) applies `gte(journalEntries.date, opts.from)` and then starts `runningBalance = Money.zero()` (journal.ts:296), never computing the balance brought forward before `from`. The UI (app/reports/general-ledger/page.tsx:306-318) labels the final figure 'Closing Balance', which is incorrect for any date-filtered run on a balance-sheet account. QB's GL/register shows a Beginning Balance row and correct running balances.
bugs.fixSketch: In `generalLedger`, when `opts.from` is set, run one extra aggregate query before the main line query: select `accountId, sum(debit), sum(credit)` from journalEntryLines joined to journalEntries, filtered by companyId, status='posted', `lt(journalEntries.date, opts.from)`, and `inArray(accountId, accountIds)`, grouped by accountId. For each account, compute `opening = balanceDelta(acct.type, sumDebit, sumCredit)` (same debit-normal/credit-normal helper already used per line) and seed `runningBalance = opening` instead of `Money.zero()`. Add an `openingBalance: string` field to `GeneralLedgerResult` (defaulting to '0.00' when no `from`), and have app/reports/general-ledger/page.tsx render a "Beginning Balance" row above the register (mirroring the existing customer-statement pattern in lib/services/statements.ts:76-110 and its page). Add a test in journal.test.ts that queries with `from` after a prior-period entry and asserts the first runningBalance and closingBalance include the brought-forward amount.

bugs.title: Budget vs Actual semantics broken: income and expenses summed into one total; no favorable/unfavorable variance; balance-sheet budget lines compare to zero
bugs.evidence: budgets.ts:269-292 — `totalBudget`/`totalActual`/`totalVariance` add revenue and expense accounts together (both as positive natural balances), making the totals and total variance meaningless. Variance is uniformly `actual - budget` (line 275) with no orientation (over-budget expense and over-target income look identical). Actuals come only from profitAndLoss (lines 247-253), so budget lines saved against balance-sheet accounts (setBudgetLine validates existence only, lines 146-150) silently report actual = 0. AUDIT_REMEDIATION.md:74 acknowledges this (#37) as deferred — confirmed still unfixed in code.
bugs.fixSketch: In budgetVsActual, join each budgeted account's type (already fetched at lines 257-260 — add `type: accounts.type` to the select). Reject or separately bucket non-P&L account types: simplest is to have setBudgetLine validate `account.type in ('revenue','expense')` and have budgetVsActual skip (or flag) legacy lines on other types. Add `accountType` and `favorable: boolean` to BudgetVsActualRow, computing favorability as `actual >= budget` for revenue and `actual <= budget` for expense while keeping the raw signed variance. Replace the single totals with sectioned totals: `incomeBudget/incomeActual/incomeVariance`, `expenseBudget/expenseActual/expenseVariance`, plus a net line (`netBudget = incomeBudget - expenseBudget`, same for actual, netVariance = netActual - netBudget) so the bottom line reads like a P&L. Update the budget report page/API consumers and budgets.test.ts to assert the sectioned totals and favorability orientation (e.g., over-budget expense yields favorable=false, over-target income favorable=true), and mark #37 resolved in AUDIT_REMEDIATION.md.

bugs.title: Budget vs Actual by Class silently drops actuals that have no matching budget line
bugs.evidence: classReports.ts:347-368 — the result loop iterates only `budgetMap` keys; actual amounts accumulated in `actualMap` (lines 329-340) for (account, class) pairs without a budget line never appear, so `totalActual` understates real activity and unbudgeted classes vanish. QB shows actual columns with $0 budget. Same income+expense total-mixing issue as budgets.ts also applies (lines 355-356).
bugs.fixSketch: In budgetVsActualByClass (lib/services/classReports.ts), (1) widen the actuals query: drop the inArray(...budgetAccountIds) filter and instead restrict to P&L accounts (join accounts and filter type IN ('revenue','expense'), companyId-scoped), so unbudgeted accounts/classes are captured; (2) load accountMeta and classNameMap for the union of ids referenced by budget lines AND actual rows; (3) build result rows from the union of budgetMap and actualMap keys — for actual-only keys emit budget '0.00', actual from actualMap, variance = actual - 0 — keeping the existing keyOf(accountId, classId??UNCLASSIFIED_ID) convention; (4) for totals, either track totalIncomeBudget/totalIncomeActual and totalExpenseBudget/totalExpenseActual separately (aligning with the deferred #37 redesign) or compute net totals as revenue minus expense rather than summing mixed natural balances. Add a test seeding a posted journal entry for an account/class with no budget line and assert the row appears with budget 0.00 and that totalActual includes it.

bugs.title: Inventory Valuation report values FIFO-tracked items at average cost, so it misstates value and cannot tie to GL inventory
bugs.evidence: inventoryValuation (inventory.ts:318-341) computes quantityOnHand × averageCost for ALL active items with no costingMethod check, while FIFO items are costed in inventoryLayers (lib/services/fifo.ts — fifo.ts never maintains items.averageCost; grep for averageCost in fifo.ts returns nothing). A separate fifoValuation (fifo.ts:350) exists but is not merged, so the valuation summary is wrong for FIFO items and there is no single report that ties to GL 1300 (AUDIT_REMEDIATION.md defers #45/#48 valuation drift).
bugs.fixSketch: In `inventoryValuation` (lib/services/inventory.ts:318), first query distinct FIFO-tracked itemIds for the company from `inventoryLayers` (same predicate the existing `assertNotFifoTracked` guard uses). For those items, compute value from remaining layers — sum of quantityRemaining × unitCost per item (reuse/extract the aggregation already written in `fifoValuation`, fifo.ts:350-409) — and report that as the item's totalValue (optionally with an effective unit cost = totalValue/qty and a `costingMethod: 'fifo' | 'average'` field on InventoryValuationRow). Keep qty × averageCost only for non-layered items, and sum both groups into the single grandTotal so the one report ties to GL 1300. Add a test that calls receiveStock twice at different unit costs, then asserts inventoryValuation's grandTotal equals the layer sum and the GL 1300 balance, and that an avg-cost item in the same company still values correctly.

bugs.title: General Ledger report omits deactivated accounts entirely, so GL no longer reconciles to the journal/trial balance
bugs.evidence: journal.ts:233 — `acctConds` always includes `eq(accounts.isActive, true)`, so all historical posted lines on a deactivated account disappear from the all-accounts GL output (and requesting that accountId directly throws notFound at journal.ts:248-250), while those same lines still feed trialBalance/profitAndLoss in reports.ts which join lines to accounts without an isActive filter.
bugs.fixSketch: In `generalLedger` (lib/services/journal.ts:228-250), drop `eq(accounts.isActive, true)` from `acctConds` at line 233 and add `isActive: accounts.isActive` to the selected columns so the report can label inactive accounts. When `opts.accountId` is provided, the existing notFound check at 248-250 then correctly fires only for genuinely missing/foreign accounts rather than deactivated ones. To keep the all-accounts report uncluttered, in the result loop (around line 294) skip accounts where `!acct.isActive && acctLines.length === 0` (active accounts can keep their current zero-line behavior), and append a marker such as "(inactive)" or an `isActive` field on `GeneralLedgerResult` for the UI. Add a regression test: post an entry hitting an account, zero it back out (or force-deactivate with a balance), deactivate, then assert generalLedger still returns those lines and that GL line totals equal trialBalance totals.

gaps.title: No Rebuild Data utility — verify exists but is read-only with no repair action
gaps.qbFeature: File > Utilities > Rebuild Data (companion to Verify Data)

bugs.title: Backup download and restore API is completely unauthenticated (full data exfiltration/overwrite)
bugs.evidence: app/api/backup/route.ts:41-47 — GET wraps getServerContext() in try/catch and proceeds to createBackup() even when auth throws FORBIDDEN ('If context resolution fails ... proceed without a name'), then returns the entire PGlite data dir as a zip. POST (route.ts:74-85) never calls getServerContext at all before restoreBackup(buffer) overwrites the data dir. middleware.ts:43 explicitly excludes /api from the session check ('matcher: /((?!api|...).*)'), and lib/context.ts:63-70 documents that API routes MUST fail closed via getServerContext because 'the API layer is not gated by middleware'. Every other domain route (qb-import, import, integrity) calls getServerContext; backup is the one route that skips it — so any unauthenticated HTTP request to the local server can download all books or wipe/replace them.
bugs.fixSketch: In GET, move getServerContext() out of the swallowing try/catch: `const ctx = await getServerContext();` at the top of the handler so a FORBIDDEN throw propagates to errorResponse() (which already maps FORBIDDEN→403 at route.ts:23), and keep only the cosmetic getCompany(ctx) name lookup inside a narrow try/catch. In POST, add `await getServerContext();` as the first statement (before reading the body) so unauthenticated restore fails closed the same way. Optionally require an owner/admin role from ctx for restore since it is destructive, and add a CSRF guard (e.g. require a custom header like `x-bka-restore: 1` or check Origin/Sec-Fetch-Site) since the route accepts a raw opaque body. Add route tests: unauthenticated GET/POST → 403; authenticated round-trip → 200.

bugs.title: Restore extracts over a live, open PGlite database and never clears the target directory
bugs.evidence: lib/services/backup.ts:78-88 — restoreBackup() does fs.mkdirSync + zip.extractAllTo(dir, overwrite=true) while the PGlite handle for that same dir stays open in the handles map (lib/db/index.ts:27,43-63). closeDb() exists (lib/db/index.ts:78-85) but is never called by the restore path (grep for closeDb shows only db.smoke.test.ts uses it). The open WASM Postgres instance can checkpoint/flush its in-memory state back onto the just-restored files at any time before the user manually restarts, silently undoing or corrupting the restore. Additionally, the target dir is not emptied first, so files present in the current DB but absent from the backup (e.g. newer pg_wal segments) survive the extract and mix two database states — pg_control from the backup plus WAL from the live DB is a corruption vector even after a clean restart. The route and UI only show a 'please restart' toast (app/api/backup/route.ts:84, app/backup/page.tsx:99-102); nothing closes the DB or relaunches.
bugs.fixSketch: Make the restore path close-then-swap: in restoreBackup(), (1) extract the zip to a sibling temp directory first and validate it looks like a PGlite data dir (e.g. contains PG_VERSION); (2) await closeDb(dir) to flush and release the live handle (make restoreBackup async, or do the close in the POST route before calling it); (3) atomically replace the data dir — fs.rmSync(dir, {recursive:true, force:true}) then fs.renameSync(tmpDir, dir) (or rename old dir aside as a safety copy and delete after success) so no stale pg_wal/control files survive; (4) either reopen via getDb(dir) so no restart is needed, or set a module-level "restore pending" flag that getDb/openDb checks to refuse reopening that dir until the process restarts, and have the Electron main process relaunch (app.relaunch() + app.exit()) on an IPC message sent after a successful restore. Add a test that restores while a handle is open and asserts the restored data is what queries see afterward.

bugs.title: Restore accepts any zip with no validation — a wrong or junk file silently 'succeeds' and bricks the company file
bugs.evidence: lib/services/backup.ts:84-88 — restoreBackup() builds AdmZip(buffer) and extractAllTo(overwrite=true) with no manifest, no PG_VERSION/pg_control presence check, no app-version or schema-version check, and no verification that the archive is a BookKeeper backup at all (the UI even accepts plain .zip — app/backup/page.tsx:157 accept=".bka,.zip"). A user restoring the wrong file gets {restored:true} and a success toast ('Restore complete', page.tsx:99-102), then an unbootable or mixed-state data dir after restart. QB Desktop validates .qbb integrity before restoring.
bugs.fixSketch: Two-part fix in lib/services/backup.ts. (1) createBackup: write a `bookkeeper-manifest.json` entry into the zip ({app:'bookkeeper-ai', formatVersion, schemaVersion/appVersion, companyName, createdAt}). (2) restoreBackup: before touching the data dir, open the zip and validate — reject if AdmZip can't parse it, if the manifest entry is missing/unparseable or `app !== 'bookkeeper-ai'`, or if formatVersion is newer than supported; for backward-compat with pre-manifest .bka files, alternatively accept archives whose entry list contains PGlite markers (`PG_VERSION` and `base/` or `global/pg_control`). Throw ServiceError('VALIDATION', 'Not a BookKeeper backup file') so the existing route maps it to a 400 the UI already surfaces (page.tsx:95). Then make the restore atomic instead of an in-place overwrite-merge: extract to a temp sibling dir, verify extraction succeeded, rename the current data dir to `<dir>.pre-restore-<ts>` (automatic rollback point), and rename the temp dir into place — so a half-failed restore can never leave a mixed-state company file. Add backup.test.ts covering: junk bytes → VALIDATION, random valid zip → VALIDATION, round-trip createBackup→restoreBackup → success, and stale-file removal (file present before restore but absent from backup is gone after).

bugs.title: Electron File-menu items (Backup Company, New/Open Company, Import Bank File) are dead — no renderer listener
bugs.evidence: electron/main.js:99-103 sends 'menu' IPC events ('new-company', 'open-company', 'import', 'backup') and electron/preload.js exposes window.bookkeeper.onMenu, but no file in app/ or components/ ever calls onMenu (grep for 'onMenu' across app/, components/, lib/ matches only preload.js; the only window.bookkeeper usages are openFile in app/banking/page.tsx:414 and app/qb-import/page.tsx:10). Clicking File > Backup Company… (or New/Open Company, Import Bank File) in the desktop build does nothing. Same for the 'update-ready' event at main.js:199.
bugs.fixSketch: Add a small client component, e.g. components/DesktopBridge.tsx ('use client'), mounted once in the root layout or AppShell. In a useEffect it checks `window.bookkeeper?.isDesktop` and subscribes: `onNavigate(route => router.push(route))` and `onMenu(action => { switch(action) { case 'import': router.push('/banking'); break; case 'backup': router.push('/backup'); break; case 'new-company': case 'open-company': router.push('/settings' /* or a company-switcher page once it exists */); break; case 'update-ready': toast('Update downloaded — restart to apply'); } })` — the /backup page (app/backup/page.tsx + app/api/backup/route.ts) and /banking import flow already exist, so routing is enough. Also update preload.js so onMenu/onNavigate return an unsubscribe function (`const h=(_e,a)=>cb(a); ipcRenderer.on('menu',h); return ()=>ipcRenderer.removeListener('menu',h)`) and return that from the useEffect cleanup, preventing duplicate handlers across React remounts/HMR.

bugs.title: IIF account import silently drops accounts on derived-code collisions and reports no per-row errors
bugs.evidence: qbImport.ts:537-544 — sanitiseCode() truncates the account name to 20 chars; qbImport.ts:253-261 treats any existing account with the same derived code as a duplicate and increments counts.skipped. Two distinct QB accounts whose first 20 sanitized chars match (common with long 'Parent:Subaccount' IIF names) import only the first; the second vanishes into an undifferentiated 'skipped' count with no name/reason reported (ImportCounts at qbImport.ts:228-233 has no error detail), so users cannot tell a real duplicate from a lost account. Validation failures from createCustomer/createVendor are likewise swallowed into 'skipped' (qbImport.ts:316-322, 359-365).
bugs.fixSketch: In importIIF (and the two CSV importers), return per-row detail alongside counts: extend the result to `{ accounts, customers, vendors, skipped, issues: Array<{ entity: 'account'|'customer'|'vendor'; name: string; code?: string; reason: 'duplicate'|'code-collision'|'validation'; message: string }> }`, pushing an issue at every `skipped++` site (qbImport.ts:254, 261, 275, 285, 296, 318, 328, 339, 361) with the row's name and the caught ServiceError message for validation failures. For the collision case specifically, stop treating a code match as a duplicate when the names differ: when the derived code already exists for a different account name, dedupe instead of skipping by truncating to 17 chars and appending '-2', '-3', ... (retrying until unique within the 20-char limit), so both accounts import; only skip when both code and name match. Pass `issues` through app/api/qb-import/route.ts unchanged and render them in app/qb-import/page.tsx as an expandable per-row list under the existing badges. Add a test importing two long account names that share the first 20 sanitized characters, asserting both are created with distinct codes and that any skipped row appears in `issues` with a reason.

bugs.title: Root route '/' is a leftover hardcoded mock dashboard — and it is the Electron start page
bugs.evidence: app/page.tsx:4-38 renders fabricated KPIs ('Revenue $2,400,000', '+8.2%'), app/page.tsx:116-131 renders five fake 'Vendor Payment $1,200.00' rows dated 2025-11-2x, and app/page.tsx:139 says 'Beautiful financial charts go here'; the search box, type filter, and 'Add Account' tile are dead controls. middleware.ts:26 explicitly allows '/' without a session, and electron/main.js:149 does mainWindow.loadURL(url) on the bare root URL, so the packaged desktop app boots straight into this fake unauthenticated dashboard (wrapped in the real sidebar, since '/' is not in AppShell's BARE list) instead of /login or /dashboard.
bugs.fixSketch: Replace the body of app/page.tsx with a tiny server component that routes by session: `import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'; import { cookies } from 'next/headers'; export default async function Home() { redirect((await cookies()).get('bka_session') ? '/dashboard' : '/login'); }` (cookie name matches middleware.ts:32; full signature verification still happens server-side as today). This deletes the fake KPIs/transactions entirely, sends the packaged Electron app (which loads the bare root URL at electron/main.js:149) to /login on first run and /dashboard thereafter, and needs no middleware or AppShell changes — though optionally drop the `pathname === '/'` allowance at middleware.ts:26 so '/' falls under the normal auth redirect for defense in depth.

bugs.title: All Electron application-menu actions are dead — no renderer ever subscribes to the IPC events
bugs.evidence: electron/main.js:99-103 (File > New Company, Open Company, Import Bank File, Backup Company), :113-116 (Reports submenu via navigate()), and :199 (update-ready) all emit via webContents.send('menu'|'navigate', ...), and preload.js:13-14 exposes onMenu/onNavigate — but a project-wide grep for onMenu|onNavigate|window.bookkeeper shows the only renderer uses of the bridge are openFile in app/banking/page.tsx:414 and app/qb-import/page.tsx. No component ever calls window.bookkeeper.onMenu or onNavigate, so every menu item in the native menu bar (and the auto-update notification) silently does nothing.
bugs.fixSketch: Add a small client component, e.g. components/DesktopBridge.tsx ('use client'), that renders null and is mounted once in app/layout.tsx. In a useEffect it checks window.bookkeeper and wires: bookkeeper.onNavigate(route => router.push(route)) using next/navigation's useRouter; and bookkeeper.onMenu(action => { switch(action) { case 'import': router.push('/banking'); break; case 'backup': router.push('/backup' /* or call the existing backup export endpoint + bookkeeper.saveFile */); break; case 'new-company': router.push('/setup' or open the company wizard); case 'open-company': router.push('/companies' or the switcher); case 'update-ready': show a toast/banner ("Update downloaded — restart to apply"); } }). Also harden preload.js: have onMenu/onNavigate return an unsubscribe function (const h = (_e,a)=>cb(a); ipcRenderer.on(ch,h); return ()=>ipcRenderer.removeListener(ch,h)) and call it in the effect cleanup, so Strict Mode/dev remounts don't stack duplicate listeners and fire actions multiple times. Add a TS declaration for window.bookkeeper (global.d.ts) so the bridge is typed.

bugs.title: Launch-time recurring/memorized-transaction run always fails with 403 in the packaged app
bugs.evidence: electron/main.js:156-166 (runDueRecurring) POSTs to /api/recurring/run from the main process with no session cookie. The route calls getServerContext() (app/api/recurring/run/route.ts:34), and lib/context.ts:71-77 throws ServiceError('FORBIDDEN') for session-less calls once any user exists — which is always true after first signup. The .catch(() => {}) swallows the 403, so the advertised 'generate any due recurring transactions on launch' behavior never works for real installs and nobody is told.
bugs.fixSketch: Give the launch-time run a legitimate system identity instead of an anonymous HTTP call. Simplest robust option: in electron/main.js startNextServer, generate a per-launch random secret (crypto.randomBytes) and pass it to the child server as BKA_INTERNAL_TOKEN; have runDueRecurring send it as an `x-bka-internal` header, and in app/api/recurring/run/route.ts (or a small helper in lib/context.ts) accept that header — when it matches process.env.BKA_INTERNAL_TOKEN, build a system ServiceContext directly (db + first/selected company + that company's ownerId) instead of calling the cookie-based getServerContext. Since the server is bound to 127.0.0.1 and the token never leaves the machine, this doesn't reopen the impersonation hole that the fail-closed fix in lib/context.ts addressed. Alternatively, skip HTTP entirely and run `runDue` from a server-startup hook (e.g., Next instrumentation.ts) with the same system context. In either case, replace the silent `.catch(() => {})` with a log (and ideally a renderer notification listing generated documents) so failures are visible.

bugs.title: Dashboard KPIs labeled 'YTD' actually show all-time totals
bugs.evidence: app/dashboard/page.tsx:13 calls profitAndLoss(ctx) with no date range, and lib/services/reports.ts:100-109 only applies date conditions when range.from/range.to are provided — so income/net figures span the company's entire history. The cards are labeled 'Revenue (YTD)' and 'Net Profit (YTD)' (app/dashboard/page.tsx:23-24), which materially misstates the numbers in any company with more than one fiscal year.
bugs.fixSketch: In app/dashboard/page.tsx, compute the year-to-date window and pass it to the report: e.g. `const now = new Date(); const yearStart = new Date(now.getFullYear(), 0, 1);` then `profitAndLoss(ctx, { from: yearStart, to: now })`. For correctness with non-calendar fiscal years, derive the period start from the company's `fiscalYearEnd` setting (lib/db/schema.ts:45) — start = day after the most recent fiscal year end — falling back to Jan 1 when unset. No change is needed in lib/services/reports.ts (its optional-range behavior is correct and relied on by app/reports/profit-loss/page.tsx); alternatively, if all-time figures were intended, relabel the cards, but passing a real YTD range is the better fix for a QuickBooks-style dashboard. Add a unit/integration test that posts one entry dated in a prior year and one in the current year and asserts the dashboard's P&L call excludes the prior-year amount.

bugs.title: Global search results never link to the found record — always to the unfiltered list page
bugs.evidence: app/api/search/route.ts:40-45 maps every hit to a static href ('/customers', '/vendors', '/items', '/invoices') with no id, query param, or filter. Searching 'Invoice #1042' in the Ctrl+K palette finds the invoice but lands the user on the full invoices list with no highlight or filter, defeating the purpose of search (QB's Find opens the actual transaction).
bugs.fixSketch: The record ids are already selected in app/api/search/route.ts — stop discarding them. Return them in the payload (e.g. { type, label, href: `/invoices?focus=${i.id}`, id }) for all four entity types. Then add a small client-side hook (e.g. useFocusParam) used by each of the four list pages: on mount, read useSearchParams().get('focus'); once the page's record list has loaded, find the matching record and auto-open its existing detail/edit modal (these pages already manage records via modals since no [id] routes exist), plus scroll-to and briefly highlight the row as a fallback. Update CommandPalette's SearchResult type only if it needs the id separately; otherwise the href change alone carries it. Total change: ~6 lines in the API route, one shared ~20-line hook, and one hook call per list page; add a test asserting /api/search hrefs contain the matched record's id.

bugs.title: Command palette destination list is out of sync with the sidebar despite claiming otherwise
bugs.evidence: components/CommandPalette.tsx:8 says 'Flat list of navigable destinations (kept in sync with the sidebar)' but the list at lines 9-25 is missing sidebar entries Mileage (/mileage), Sales Reps (/sales-reps), Inventory Ops (/inventory-ops), Estimate Follow-up (/estimates-followup), 1099 E-File (/reports/1099-efile), P&L Comparative, P&L by Month, Balance Sheet (Cash), and P&L by Class (all present in AppShell.tsx:30,77-80,92-93,101,104). It also omits pages that exist but are in neither nav: /budgets (only reachable via a card on /reports, app/reports/page.tsx:125) and /transactions (app/transactions/page.tsx — orphaned, no href to it anywhere in the codebase).
bugs.fixSketch: Eliminate the duplicated list by deriving the palette from the sidebar's single source of truth: move the navGroups array out of components/AppShell.tsx into a shared module (e.g. lib/nav.ts exporting navGroups), have AppShell import it, and in CommandPalette.tsx replace the hand-maintained DESTINATIONS literal with `const DESTINATIONS: Dest[] = navGroups.flatMap(g => g.links.map(l => ({ label: l.label, href: l.path, group: 'Go to' })))` plus a small appended array of intentionally non-sidebar destinations such as ['Budgets', '/budgets'] and ['Transactions', '/transactions'] (and decide whether /transactions should instead get a sidebar/reports link or be removed since it is currently unreachable). Optionally add a unit test asserting every navGroups path appears in the palette destinations so future sidebar additions cannot silently drift again.

bugs.title: Offline desktop app loads its UI font from Google Fonts CDN
bugs.evidence: app/layout.tsx:14-16 links to https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter... The product is explicitly an offline desktop app (electron/main.js:8 'keeps all financial data on the user's machine (offline)'), so on a machine without internet the brand font silently fails and typography degrades; it also makes every app launch ping Google. The font should be bundled (next/font/local or a file in public/).
bugs.fixSketch: Remove the three Google Fonts <link> tags from app/layout.tsx (lines 14-16) and self-host Inter. Simplest path: `import { Inter } from "next/font/google"` with `subsets: ["latin"], weight: ["400","600","700","900"], variable: "--font-inter"` — next/font downloads the woff2 at BUILD time and serves it from the app bundle, so runtime is fully offline with zero Google requests. If builds must also be network-free, instead drop the Inter woff2 files (variable font, weights 400-900) into public/fonts/ and use `next/font/local` (or plain @font-face rules in globals.css pointing at /fonts/inter-var.woff2). Apply the font via `<html className={inter.variable}>` or `inter.className` on <body>, and update the two hard-coded stacks in app/globals.css (lines 18 and 36) to `var(--font-inter), Arial, sans-serif` so the bundled font is actually used. This matches the project's own IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md:95, which already prescribes next/font.

bugs.title: Modals cannot be closed with Escape and have no focus trap
bugs.evidence: components/ui.tsx:127-156 — the shared Modal renders a backdrop with onClick and an X button but registers no keydown handler, no role='dialog', no aria-modal, and no focus management. The only global Escape handler belongs to the command palette (components/CommandPalette.tsx:39). Every data-entry dialog in the app (invoices, estimates, companies, backup restore) traps keyboard users.
bugs.fixSketch: Fix it once in the shared Modal (components/ui.tsx). Add a React.useEffect (gated on `open`) that registers a document keydown listener calling onClose() on e.key === 'Escape', and removes it on cleanup. Give the Card wrapper role="dialog", aria-modal="true", and aria-labelledby pointing at the h2 title (give it an id). For focus management: on open, store document.activeElement in a ref, then focus the dialog container (a ref with tabIndex={-1}); on unmount/close, restore focus to the stored element. Implement a minimal focus trap in the same keydown handler: on Tab, query the container for focusable elements ('a[href], button:not([disabled]), input, select, textarea, [tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])') and wrap focus from last to first (and Shift+Tab first to last). Optionally render through ReactDOM.createPortal(document.body) so stacking contexts can't clip it. Because all 35+ pages consume this single component, one ~30-line change fixes Escape-close, dialog semantics, and keyboard trapping app-wide; add a React Testing Library test asserting Escape fires onClose and that Tab cycles within the modal.

bugs.title: Sidebar and palette navigation use full-page loads instead of client-side routing
bugs.evidence: components/AppShell.tsx:160-169 renders sidebar links as plain <a href> elements and components/CommandPalette.tsx:84 navigates via window.location.href, while pages elsewhere use next/link (e.g. app/settings/page.tsx:190). Every sidebar click tears down and re-bootstraps the entire React app against the local server — visibly slower navigation and lost in-page state in a desktop shell where Next's client router is available for free.
bugs.fixSketch: In components/AppShell.tsx, import Link from 'next/link' and replace the `<a key={nav.label} href={nav.path} ...>` element at lines 160-169 with `<Link key={nav.label} href={nav.path} ...>` (same className/children; Link accepts className directly in app router). In components/CommandPalette.tsx, import `useRouter` from 'next/navigation', call `const router = useRouter()` in the component, and change `go()` (line 82-85) to `setOpen(false); router.push(href);` — keeping `window.location.href` only for the cross-auth logout redirect in AppShell.tsx:133, which legitimately needs a full reload to drop client state. Optionally add `prefetch={false}` on the ~50 sidebar Links if prefetching all routes against the local PGlite-backed server proves heavy, and verify active-link highlighting still works (it will, since it derives from usePathname).

gaps.title: RBAC exists but is never enforced — every company member can do everything
gaps.qbFeature: QuickBooks Desktop user roles/permissions (restrict users to AR-only, AP-only, view-only, etc.)
gaps.description: lib/services/rbac.ts implements getRole/requireRole/requireWrite and userCompanies stores a role ('owner'|'admin'|'accountant'|'viewer'), but a repo-wide grep shows requireRole/requireWrite are called ONLY from rbac.test.ts — zero call sites in any of the 144 API routes or 73 services. middleware.ts:41-44 explicitly excludes /api, and getServerContext (lib/context.ts) only verifies membership, never role. A 'viewer' member can post journal entries, void invoices, run payroll, and close periods. TODO.md claims RBAC done (wave 6-8); it is half-built (storage without enforcement).
gaps.whereToBuild: Call requireWrite/requireRole from lib/services/_base.ts helpers or per-route in app/api/**/route.ts; roles already in lib/services/rbac.ts + lib/db/schema.ts (userCompanies.role)

gaps.title: No way to void/delete/unapply a received payment or bill payment
gaps.qbFeature: QuickBooks Desktop Edit > Delete/Void Payment and editing payment applications in Receive Payments / Pay Bills
gaps.description: voidInvoice (lib/services/invoices.ts:559-564) and voidBill (bills.ts:229-234) refuse to void documents with payments applied and tell the user to 'Unapply payments first' — but no unapply operation exists anywhere: grep for unapply/deletePayment/voidPayment across the repo finds only those error messages; lib/services/payments.ts and billPayments.ts export only create/list/get; app/api/payments and app/api/bill-payments have no [id] route and no DELETE handler. Consequence: any invoice or bill that has ever received a payment can NEVER be voided, and an erroneous payment (wrong amount, wrong customer) is permanently posted to the GL with no correction path other than a manual journal entry that desyncs the AR/AP subledger.
gaps.whereToBuild: lib/services/payments.ts + billPayments.ts (voidPayment/voidBillPayment reversing the JE via voidJournalEntry and rolling back invoice/bill amountPaid/balanceDue), plus app/api/payments/[id]/route.ts and app/api/bill-payments/[id]/route.ts

gaps.title: Inventory is not integrated with invoices or bills — no automatic COGS or quantity relief on sale, no item receipt on purchase
gaps.qbFeature: QuickBooks Desktop inventory items on sales forms (auto COGS + quantity decrement) and Items tab on bills/item receipts (quantity + average-cost increase)
gaps.description: createInvoice (lib/services/invoices.ts:142-498) posts AR/income/tax only — it never calls recordCOGS or the FIFO module and never touches items.quantityOnHand even when a line references an inventory item. Grep confirms recordCOGS is invoked only from the manual POST /api/inventory route (app/api/inventory/route.ts:112) and tests. On the purchase side, BillLineInput (lib/services/bills.ts:30-38) has only accountId/amount — there is no itemId, so receiving inventory via a bill cannot increase quantity on hand or update average cost. A user selling an inventory item on an invoice gets revenue with no COGS and unchanged stock unless they remember to make a separate manual inventory transaction, so margins, inventory valuation, and the 1300 GL account silently drift from reality.
gaps.whereToBuild: lib/services/invoices.ts (call recordCOGS / fifo consumption per inventory line inside the createInvoice transaction; reverse on void), lib/services/bills.ts (item-based bill lines that increase QOH/avg cost), lib/db schema for bill item lines

gaps.title: Employer payroll taxes (employer FICA match, FUTA) are never computed or recorded
gaps.qbFeature: QuickBooks Desktop after-the-fact payroll: employer-share Social Security/Medicare + FUTA accrual, and Form 941 lines 5a/5c (taxable wages x combined 12.4%/2.9% rates)
gaps.description: runPaycheck (lib/services/payroll.ts:154-320) posts only Dr Payroll Expense (gross) / Cr Liabilities (employee withholdings + deductions) / Cr Checking (net). The PaycheckLineInput kind 'employer_contribution' is declared (payroll.ts:42) but there is no input field, no computation, and lineValues (payroll.ts:231-250) never includes it. form941Data (lib/services/payrollReports.ts:218-228) sums only employee-side withholding lines, so the 941 worksheet reports half the actual FICA owed. Payroll Liabilities (2300) and payroll expense are systematically understated by the employer share, so 'pay payroll liabilities' amounts will never match actual federal deposits. This is in-scope for simple after-the-fact payroll (record liabilities + 941-style worksheets).
gaps.whereToBuild: lib/services/payroll.ts (compute employer FICA/FUTA in runPaycheck, post Dr Payroll Tax Expense / Cr 2300), lib/services/payrollReports.ts (941 employer share), lib/services/payrollTax.ts

gaps.title: Zod validation declared as an architecture rule but never implemented — mutating routes pass raw JSON into services
gaps.qbFeature: Input integrity on every transaction form (QBD validates field types/ranges at entry)
gaps.description: TODO.md line 102 declares 'Validation -> zod at every service boundary. Already a dependency' and item 0.6 (lib/validation/ shared zod schemas) is marked [~] incomplete. zod@3.24.1 is in package.json dependencies, but a repo-wide grep finds zero zod imports in app/ or lib/ (only package.json/TODO.md mention it) and lib/validation/ does not exist. Mutating routes forward req.json() bodies directly, e.g. app/api/accounts/route.ts:41-42 passes the raw body to createAccount (which accepts an arbitrary openingBalance — see bug below), and most routes only hand-check 2-3 required fields. Unrecognized/garbage fields (negative quantities as strings, malformed dates producing Invalid Date, extreme decimals) reach Drizzle and either 500 or persist inconsistent data.
gaps.whereToBuild: lib/validation/ (shared zod schemas per service input type), applied in app/api/**/route.ts POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE handlers or at the top of each lib/services/* mutation

gaps.title: No protection for reconciled transactions and no Undo Last Reconciliation
gaps.qbFeature: QuickBooks Desktop warns/blocks when editing or voiding a cleared (reconciled) transaction, and offers Undo Last Reconciliation
gaps.description: voidJournalEntry (lib/services/posting.ts:163-204) checks only assertPeriodOpen — it never checks whether the entry's bank lines were cleared in a completed reconciliation, so voiding a reconciled deposit/check succeeds silently. The next reconciliation then seeds its cleared balance from bankAccounts.lastReconciledBalance (lib/services/reconcile.ts:102-117), which still includes the voided line, producing an unexplainable beginning-balance discrepancy with no tooling to detect or repair it: reconcile.ts has no undoReconciliation and app/api/reconciliations has no delete/reopen for completed sessions.
gaps.whereToBuild: lib/services/posting.ts (guard in voidJournalEntry via reconciliationItems lookup), lib/services/reconcile.ts (undoLastReconciliation restoring lastReconciledBalance/Date and deleting the session's items)

gaps.title: Year-end close hardcodes calendar year, ignoring the company's fiscalYearEnd setting
gaps.qbFeature: QuickBooks Desktop fiscal-year-aware automatic closing (company file fiscal year start month)
gaps.description: yearEndClose (lib/services/fiscalClose.ts:55-56, 201) fixes the close window to Jan 1 - Dec 31 and posts the closing entry on Dec 31, while updateCompany (lib/services/company.ts:99-101) stores a settings.fiscalYearEnd that nothing in fiscalClose.ts reads. A company with a June 30 fiscal year cannot produce a correct retained-earnings rollover.
gaps.whereToBuild: lib/services/fiscalClose.ts (derive year window from company.settings.fiscalYearEnd), app/api/year-end-close/route.ts

bugs.title: Multi-line foreign-currency invoices throw UNBALANCED and cannot be created (per-line FX rounding)
bugs.evidence: invoices.ts:407-410 defines toBase() = round2(txAmount * exchangeRate) and applies it independently to the A/R debit (line 430), retainage debit (436), each income-account credit (445), tax credit (454) and discount debit (463). The identity 'debits == credits' only holds pre-rounding; sum of independently rounded products differs from the rounded sum. Empirically reproduced: a 2-line EUR invoice (0.55 to 4000 + 0.55 to 4100) at exchangeRate 1.5 throws ServiceError 'Entry is out of balance: debits 1.65 ≠ credits 1.66' from assertBalanced (posting.ts:81) via invoices.ts:468 — invoice creation hard-fails for any FX invoice whose lines hit more than one income account (or use retainage/tax) with unlucky cents. Fix requires money.allocate-style penny pre-allocation, which the posting.ts:78-79 comment itself prescribes but createInvoice does not do.
bugs.fixSketch: In `createInvoice`'s posting section (invoices.ts:407-466), stop rounding each converted line independently; instead pick one base-currency anchor B = round2((subtotal + taxAmount) × exchangeRate) and use the existing `allocate()` (lib/money.ts:58, largest-remainder) to distribute B across each side of the entry: allocate B over the credit-side weights (each income account's tx-currency amount plus taxAmount) to get the income/tax credit lines, and allocate B over the debit-side weights (dueNow, retainageAmount, discount) to get the A/R, retainage, and discount-contra lines. Both sides then sum to exactly B, so `assertBalanced` always passes, and every line stays within a cent of its true converted value. Add a regression test: 2-line EUR invoice 0.55→4000 + 0.55→4100 at rate 1.5 must create successfully with a balanced trial balance (it currently throws UNBALANCED debits 1.65 ≠ credits 1.66).

bugs.title: GET/POST /api/companies is completely unauthenticated — cross-tenant company listing and rogue company creation
bugs.evidence: app/api/companies/route.ts:9-25 calls getDb()/ensureDevCompany()/listCompanies()/createCompany() directly without getServerContext or any session check (middleware.ts:43 excludes all /api routes from auth). listCompanies (lib/services/company.ts:43-45) returns full rows — id, name, ownerId, settings JSONB — for EVERY tenant in the database, so an unauthenticated caller enumerates all company files. POST creates a company owned by ensureDevCompany's first user (company.ts:79-83), letting an unauthenticated caller pollute the database (and a second authenticated user create companies assigned to user #1). This bypasses the fail-closed hardening the audit added to getServerContext (lib/context.ts:62-77) — AUDIT_REMEDIATION.md fixed /api/companies/select but not /api/companies itself.
bugs.fixSketch: Mirror the pattern already used in app/api/companies/select/route.ts. In app/api/companies/route.ts, call `const userId = await getSessionUserId()` at the top of both handlers and return 401 if absent — with one carve-out for genuine first-run: as in lib/context.ts:71-77, if no row exists in `users` (or BKA_ALLOW_DEV_FALLBACK=1), allow the ensureDevCompany path so onboarding can boot. For GET, replace listCompanies(db) with a membership-scoped query (add e.g. `listCompaniesForUser(db, userId)` in lib/services/company.ts: select from companies joined to userCompanies where userCompanies.userId = userId), so a user only sees their own company files. For POST, set `ownerId: userId` from the session instead of ensureDevCompany's first-user id. Add regression tests alongside lib/services/auditFixes.test.ts: unauthenticated GET/POST → 401 once a user exists; member A's GET does not include member B's company; POST creates the company owned by the session user.

bugs.title: Duplicate invoiceId/billId in a payment's applications bypasses the over-application guard and desyncs AR/AP control accounts from the subledger
bugs.evidence: receivePayment validates each application against the invoice's ORIGINAL balanceDue via a Map keyed by id (payments.ts:130-147), so applications [{invoiceId: X, 100}, {invoiceId: X, 100}] on a $100 invoice both pass the line-142 check. The apply loop (payments.ts:195-223) then inserts two paymentApplications rows ($200 total) and updates the invoice twice from the SAME stale snapshot (line 207: invoiceById.get(...)), leaving amountPaid=$100 — while the journal entry credited A/R for the full $200 (line 167-176). Result: A/R control account permanently understated vs open-invoice subledger by $100, with no validation error. payBills has the identical defect (billPayments.ts:149-195 validates per-row against the original balanceDue read before the transaction; billPayments.ts:243-265 applies from stale billRows snapshots) — Dr A/P $200 against a $100 bill. The API routes (app/api/payments/route.ts, app/api/bill-payments/route.ts) do not dedupe applications. AUDIT_REMEDIATION's 'per-invoice over-application rejected' fix covers only single applications, not duplicates.
bugs.fixSketch: In both receivePayment and payBills, normalize applications before validation: aggregate by invoiceId/billId (e.g. `const merged = new Map<string, Money>(); for (const app of input.applications) merged.set(app.invoiceId, (merged.get(app.invoiceId) ?? Money.zero()).plus(app.amountApplied))`) — or simpler, throw `validation('Duplicate invoiceId in applications')` when `new Set(ids).size !== ids.length`. Then run the existing per-id balanceDue check against the aggregated amount, which is now correct because each id appears exactly once. In payments.ts apply the merged map in the loop (one paymentApplications row and one invoice update per unique invoice); in billPayments.ts build billRows from the merged map the same way. As defense-in-depth, change the invoice/bill updates to SQL-relative increments (`amountPaid = amountPaid + :applied`, `balanceDue = balanceDue - :applied` via drizzle sql`` template) instead of recomputing from the in-memory snapshot, so any future duplicate-or-concurrent path still converges. Add tests: duplicate-invoiceId payment rejected (or merged), duplicate-billId payBills rejected, and an assertion that after a multi-application payment the sum of paymentApplications equals the delta in invoice.amountPaid.

bugs.title: createAccount openingBalance writes the cached balance with no offsetting journal entry — breaks double-entry and the app's own integrity check
bugs.evidence: accounts.ts:57 sets balance: toAmountString(input.openingBalance ?? 0) directly on insert with no journal entry posted (posting.ts is the only other writer of accounts.balance) and no offset to an Opening Balance Equity account (QBD behavior). The cached balance immediately diverges from the GL: integrity check #2 'Cached account.balance matches GL' (lib/services/integrity.ts:75) will flag every account created this way, GL-derived reports (trial balance, year-end close at fiscalClose.ts:59-84, liabilityPayments.creditBalance at liabilityPayments.ts:39-59) ignore the opening amount while the account list shows it, and total debits != credits across the books. Reachable from POST /api/accounts which passes the raw body straight through (app/api/accounts/route.ts:41-42). Also bypasses assertPeriodOpen (no date is even associated with the balance).
bugs.fixSketch: In createAccount, stop writing openingBalance into the cached balance directly. Insert the new account with balance '0.00'; then, if a non-zero openingBalance was supplied, post a real balanced journal entry through the existing posting service (which already updates cached balances at posting.ts:224-226): for debit-normal account types (asset/expense) debit the new account and credit an "Opening Balance Equity" account; reverse for credit-normal types (liability/equity/revenue). Find-or-create the Opening Balance Equity account per company (e.g., code 3900, type 'equity', subtype 'opening_balance_equity'), mirroring QuickBooks Desktop. Accept an optional `asOfDate` in CreateAccountInput (default today) for the entry date and run it through assertPeriodOpen, and wrap the account insert plus posting in a single DB transaction so a failed posting doesn't leave a half-created account. This keeps cached balance, GL, trial balance, year-end close, and integrity check #2 all consistent. Add a test: createAccount with openingBalance 500 yields a posted, balanced JE, cached balance 500.00, and a passing runIntegrityChecks.

bugs.title: creditMemos.applyToInvoice recomputes balanceDue from invoice.total, ignoring retainage — re-introduces the holdback and can show a positive balance on a settled invoice
bugs.evidence: creditMemos.ts:351 computes newBalance = Money.of(invoice.total).minus(newAmountPaid), whereas the audit-fixed canonical logic in invoices.markPaidAmount (invoices.ts:623-626) computes balance against the billed base (total - retainageAmount). For an invoice with total 100, retainage 10, balanceDue 90: applying a 90 credit memo yields newAmountPaid=90, newBalance=100-90=10 → status 'partial' with balanceDue 10.00 stored (line 361-363) even though the invoice is fully settled; the held-back retainage re-enters the amount due. Additionally line 362 stores Money.abs(newBalance), which would silently flip a genuinely negative balance to a positive amount due. applyToInvoice should delegate to markPaidAmount (which already exists for exactly this purpose per its docstring) instead of duplicating the math.
bugs.fixSketch: In creditMemos.applyToInvoice, replace the duplicated invoice-update math (creditMemos.ts:350-367) with a call to the existing canonical helper: import { markPaidAmount } from './invoices' and inside the inTransaction callback call `const updatedInvoice = await markPaidAmount(tx, invoice.id, toAmountString(amount))` (markPaidAmount takes a positive delta and already computes newBalance against billedBase = total - retainageAmount, sets status, and applies the abs guard only for sub-cent rounding). Keep the existing pre-checks (amount ≤ memo.unapplied, amount ≤ invoice.balanceDue, same customer, not void) and the credit-memo update/audit-write as-is. Alternatively, if avoiding the cross-service call, inline the same billedBase logic: `const billedBase = Money.round2(Money.of(invoice.total).minus(Money.of(invoice.retainageAmount ?? '0'))); const newBalance = Money.round2(billedBase.minus(newAmountPaid));`. Add a regression test in creditMemos.test.ts: create an invoice with retainagePercent (e.g. total 100, retainage 10%, balanceDue 90), apply a 90.00 credit memo, assert invoice.status === 'paid' and balanceDue === '0.00'.

bugs.title: bankCategorize.categorize/unmatch are not transactional — GL posting and matched-flag update can diverge, enabling duplicate postings
bugs.evidence: categorize posts the journal entry at bankCategorize.ts:134-140 and only afterwards, in a SEPARATE statement outside any transaction, marks the staging row matched (bankCategorize.ts:143-151); unmatch likewise voids the entry (line 263) before clearing flags (266-270) with no inTransaction wrapper. Every comparable multi-step flow in the codebase (createInvoice, createBill, receivePayment, payBills, createDeposit, createTransfer) wraps posting + document update in inTransaction. If the flag update fails (or the batch categorizeSuggested loop at lines 223-234 is interrupted), the transaction remains matched=false with a live posted entry, and re-categorizing it posts a second, duplicate GL entry; the catch-all at line 231-233 even swallows such partial failures silently.
bugs.fixSketch: In lib/services/bankCategorize.ts, wrap each multi-step flow in the existing inTransaction helper from ./_base (postJournalEntry/voidJournalEntry already accept a tx-scoped ctx, and Drizzle+PGlite nests via savepoints, exactly as billPayments/deposits/transfers already do): in categorize(), move the postJournalEntry call, the bankTransactions update (with a `eq(bankTransactions.matched,false)` guard in the WHERE plus a rowcount check to make the update itself race-safe), and the writeAudit into one `inTransaction(ctx, async (tx) => {...})`; in unmatch(), wrap voidJournalEntry + the flag-clearing update + writeAudit the same way. As belt-and-braces against any future non-transactional caller, actually persist sourceRef: add a source_ref column to journal_entries with a partial unique index on (company_id, source_ref) WHERE status != 'void', pass it through in postJournalEntry, and surface a CONFLICT ServiceError on violation. Finally, change categorizeSuggested's bare catch to at least log/collect per-transaction failures instead of swallowing them, and add a test that simulates a failure between posting and flag update asserting the entry rolls back (no orphan GL entry, matched stays false).
